case_title: AUGUSTINE FALLAY VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEPARTMENT OF BUILDING

PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD VACATE FILED BY PETITIONER FALLAY, AUGUSTINE AS TO RESPONDENT CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEPARTMENT OF BUILDING INSPECTIONS JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PETITION TO VACATE CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PETITIONER FAILED TO SERVE AND FILE HIS PETITION WITHIN 100 DAYS AFTER THE DATE OF SERVICE OF THE AWARD ON PETITIONER. (CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1288). IN ADDITION, THE COURT INDICATED THAT NOTWITHSTANDING PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT, HE WAS NOT A PARTY TO THE ARBITRATION. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
MINI-MINUTES FOR OCT-29-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
Demurrer To Petitionto Vacate Contractual Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Oct-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Demurrer To Petition to Vacate Contractual Arbitration Award.
APPEARANCES: Augustine Fallay, 510/469-5202 petitioner & opposing party in propria persona; Margaret W. Baumgartner, Deputy City Atty 554-3859 for deft & moving party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PETITIONER FAILED TO SERVE AND FILE HIS PETITION WITHIN 100 DAYS AFTER THE DATE OF SERVICE OF THE AWARD ON PETITIONER. (CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1288). In addition, the Court indicated that notwithstanding plaintiff's argument, he was not a party to the arbitration. Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
